prompt:
You are cleaning up a database of names. Each name may have inconsistent casing (ALL CAPS, all lowercase, or rAnDoM mixed case) and may have leading/trailing whitespace.

Write a function that takes a list of name strings and returns a new list where each name is normalized to Title Case (first letter of each word capitalized, rest lowercase) with leading/trailing whitespace removed.

Examples:
  normalize_names(["JOHN DOE", "jane smith", "aLiCe BoB"])
  => ["John Doe", "Jane Smith", "Alice Bob"]

  normalize_names(["  MARY JANE  ", "bob"])
  => ["Mary Jane", "Bob"]

  normalize_names([])
  => []

Input: A list of strings (0 <= len(names) <= 100). Each string contains only letters and spaces.
Output: A list of strings, each in Title Case with no leading/trailing whitespace.
validation_trace: Verified reference solution against all 8 tests: (1) 'JOHN DOE'.strip().title()='John Doe', 'jane smith'.strip().title()='Jane Smith', 'aLiCe BoB'.strip().title()='Alice Bob' ✓; (2) 'MARY'.strip().title()='Mary' ✓; (3) []->[]; (4) '  hello world  '.strip().title()='Hello World' ✓; (5) 'Already Title Case'.title()='Already Title Case' ✓; (6) 'MARY JANE WATSON'.title()='Mary Jane Watson' ✓; (7) 'a'.title()='A' ✓; (8) strip+title on each gives ['Upper','Lower','Mixed'] ✓. All pass.
